strengths: Career longevity of approximately 17-20 years technically meets the 15-year minimum experience floor.
Polyglot engineering depth across mobile, backend, geospatial, and tooling domains signals genuine technical versatility as an IC.
GISCollective demonstrates entrepreneurial initiative and ability to independently build and ship a real product.
concerns: AI experience is limited to 3 months of project-level work with no shipped AI products, no publications, no recognized community standing, and no advanced degree — failing every dimension of the role's 'demonstrated and recognized AI expertise' requirement.

Zero director-level or strategic customer advisory experience anywhere in the resume; the role's core function is advising strategic enterprise customers and shaping AI differentiation strategy, neither of which appears in this candidate's career history.

Cloud depth is surface-level consumer usage (AWS CDK at Optilyz) rather than the deep cloud architecture fluency required to credibly advise enterprise customers on cloud and AI adoption at a principal or director IC level.

key_factors.factor: Absent AI expertise and credentials: no advanced degree, no publications, no shipped AI platform, no recognized standing in the field — all explicitly required by the role.

No strategic customer advisory or executive influence experience: the entire job function centers on director-level customer engagement and strategy shaping, which is structurally absent from this candidate's career.
Long IC career tenure meets minimum experience threshold but does not compensate for missing domain and leadership dimensions.

response.justification: This candidate is a solid, experienced individual contributor whose engineering track record is genuine but misaligned with the role's requirements by approximately two organizational layers and five to seven years of domain maturity. The AI work is too recent (three months) and too narrow (project-level only) to credibly satisfy the 'recognized AI expertise' standard the role demands, and the resume contains no evidence of strategic advisory capacity, executive customer engagement, or deep cloud architecture fluency that define the position's day-to-day responsibilities. This is not a polish or framing problem — the gaps are structural: without a graduate AI credential, a history of shipping AI platforms at scale, or a track record of influencing enterprise strategy at the director level, no honest presentation of this resume closes the distance to this job description.
